title: epipactis persica subsp. moravica
intro: Epipactis persica subsp. moravica, also known as the Moravian helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe, specifically the Moravian region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Epipactis persica subsp. moravica include relieving bronchitis and asthma symptoms, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Epipactis persica subsp. moravica can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
